segments.text: Part 6B: Carbohydrates Dr. Shahir S. Rizk

Carbohydrates
Carbohydrates , called sugars and starches, are polyhydroxy aldehydes or ketones , or compounds  that can be hydrolyzed to them.
The simplest carbohydrates are monosaccharides .

Carbohydrates
Disaccharides are composed of two monosaccharide units joined together.

Carbohydrates
Disaccharides are composed of two monosaccharide units joined together.
What is an acetal?
In sugars, the R groups are connected

Carbohydrates
Disaccharides are composed of two monosaccharide units joined together.
What is an acetal?
In sugars, the R groups are connected
Hemiacetal:
If the sugar ring is broken

Carbohydrates
Polysaccharides are composed of three or more monosaccharide units joined together.

Carbohydrates
Carbohydrates are synthesized in green plants by photosynthesis , whereby energy from the sun is stored as chemical energy in carbohydrates.
In the body, they are used for bursts of energy needed during exercise in the form of glucose.

Monosaccharides
Monosaccharides , the simplest carbohydrates, generally have 3 to 6 C atoms in a chain with an aldehyde or ketone ending and many –OH groups.
Aldehyde monosaccharides are aldoses ; ketone monosaccharides are ketoses .

Monosaccharides
The simplest aldose is glyceraldehyde .
The simplest ketose is dihydroxyacetone .
They are constitutional isomers of each other, sharing the formula C 3 H 6 O 3 .
They are intermediate products of the breakdown of glucose

Monosaccharides
A monosaccharide is characterized by the number
of C atoms in its chain:
A triose has 3 C’ s.
A tetrose has 4 C’ s.
A pentose has 5 C’ s.
A hexose has 6 C’ s.
The terms are then combined with the words aldose
and ketose:
Glyceraldehyde is an aldotriose .
Dihydroxyacetone is a ketotriose .

Monosaccharides
Classify the following sugars based on the number of Carbons and the location of the carbonyl
aldotetrose .
ketopentose .
aldohexose
ketohexose

Monosaccharides
How many chirality centers are there in each of the following molecules?
2
2
4
3

Monosaccharides A. Fischer Projection Formula
All carbohydrates have 1 or more chirality centers .
Glyceraldehyde, the simplest aldose, has one chirality center, and has two possible enantiomers .

Monosaccharides A. Fischer Projection Formula
The prefix D is used when the –OH group is drawn on the right side of the carbon chain.
The prefix L is used when the –OH group is drawn on the left side of the carbon chain.
The wedged and dashed lines can be re-drawn in a Fischer projection formula:

Monosaccharides B. More than One Chirality Center
Glucose has four chirality centers and is drawn as:

Monosaccharides B. More than One Chirality Center
The configuration of the chirality center farthest from the carbonyl group determines whether a monosaccharide is D or L .

Monosaccharides B. More than One Chirality Center
All naturally occurring sugars are D sugars.

Monosaccharides C. Common Monosaccharides
Glucose (dextrose) is blood sugar and the most abundant monosaccharide.
Excess glucose is stored as the polysaccharide glycogen or converted to fat .
Normal blood glucose levels are 70-110 mg/dL .

Monosaccharides C. Common Monosaccharides
Insulin regulates blood glucose levels by stimulating the uptake of glucose into tissues or the formation of glycogen.
Patients with diabetes produce insufficient insulin to adequately regulate blood sugar levels, so they  must monitor their diet and/or inject insulin daily .

GLUT-4: glucose transported
Mostly found in muscle and fat cells
Membrane protein stored in vesicles within the cell
Insulin triggers fusion of the vesicle with the cell membrane  glucose transport into the cell
Monosaccharides C. Common Monosaccharides

Monosaccharides C. Common Monosaccharides
Galactose is one of the components of the disaccharide lactose .
Patients with galactosemia lack an enzyme needed to metabolize galactose, which accumulates and causes cataracts and cirrhosis.
Fructose is one of the components of the disaccharide sucrose .
It is a ketohexose found in honey and almost twice as sweet as table sugar with the same number of calories per gram.

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides
When an aldehyde reacts with an alcohol, a hemiacetal is formed (Section 16.8):

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides
When the aldehyde and alcohol are on the same molecule, a stable cyclic hemiacetal is formed:
Is Carbon #1 a chirality center?
No, it has a double bond
OH becomes –O– and the C=O becomes C–OH

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides
The C atom that is part of the hemiacetal is a new chirality center , called the anomeric carbon.

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides A. The Cyclic Forms of D -Glucose
To cyclize D -glucose, first we must determine which alcohol to use to make a six-membered ring.

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides The Cyclic Forms of D -Glucose
The first step in cyclization is to rotate glucose 90 o .

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides A. The Cyclic Forms of D -Glucose
Next, the chain must be twisted around, forming a six-membered ring:

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides A. The Cyclic Forms of D -Glucose
As the reaction occurs, there are two cyclic forms of D -glucose, an a anomer and a b anomer .
These rings are called Haworth projections .

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides A. The Cyclic Forms of D -Glucose
The cyclization reaction exists in an equilibrium called mutarotation .

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides C. The Cycle Forms of Fructose
Ketohexoses like fructose form five-membered rings with two anomers.
The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides C. The Cycle Forms of Fructose

The Cyclic Forms of Monosaccharides C. The Cycle Forms of Fructose
Summary of carbohydrate cyclization:
A new chirality center is created ( anomeric Carbon )
OH becomes C–O–C and the C=O becomes C–OH Creating a hemiacetal
Anomeric Carbon can be α or β
The cyclization reaction exists in an equilibrium called mutarotation .
